provimento-213-cnj-desafios-tecnologia-c.title: CNJ Provision 213 and the technological challenges for Brazilian notary offices
provimento-213-cnj-desafios-tecnologia-c.excerpt: Understand how CNJ Provision 213 impacts notary office IT infrastructure and what measures are needed to ensure compliance and security.
provimento-213-cnj-desafios-tecnologia-c.metaTitle: CNJ Provision 213: IT Challenges for Notary Offices | WMTi
provimento-213-cnj-desafios-tecnologia-c.metaDescription: Understand how CNJ Provision 213 impacts notary office IT infrastructure and what measures are needed for compliance and security.

sections.body: The digital transformation has been profoundly impacting the operation of notary offices in Brazil. In recent years, the National Council of Justice (CNJ) has published several provisions aimed at modernizing, standardizing, and increasing the security of services provided by notary offices. Among these initiatives, Provision 213 represents an important milestone by reinforcing guidelines related to technological modernization, system integration, and information security in notarial and registry activities. For many notary offices, implementing these guidelines brings a major challenge: adapting technological infrastructure to ensure compliance, security, and operational continuity.

sections.heading: What is CNJ Provision 213
sections.body: CNJ Provision 213 establishes guidelines for technological modernization and integration of notary services, reinforcing the need for digitization, system interoperability, and information security measures. The central objective is to ensure that Brazilian notary offices operate within technological standards capable of offering: greater legal certainty, greater operational efficiency, standardization of procedures, system integration, and protection of data and digital documents.

sections.heading: The importance of IT infrastructure in notary offices
sections.body: Notary offices deal daily with sensitive information and documents of high legal value. Any technological failure can generate serious impacts, such as system unavailability, data loss, compromise of digital documents, service interruption to the public, and legal and operational risks. That is why information technology infrastructure has become one of the pillars for the secure operation of notary offices.

sections.heading: Information security: a growing requirement
sections.body: Another essential point related to Provision 213 is the need to ensure high levels of information security. Notary offices work with public records, personal data, and electronic documents that need to be protected against cyber attacks, system intrusions, information leaks, operational failures, and file loss. Solutions such as corporate firewalls, enterprise antivirus, network monitoring, and digital security policies become essential.

sections.heading: Operational continuity and technological stability
sections.body: Another challenge faced by many notary offices is ensuring that systems function continuously and stably. System unavailability can cause service paralysis, procedural delays, operational losses, and user dissatisfaction. For this reason, notary offices must rely on reliable servers, structured networks, and specialized technical support.

sections.heading: Technological modernization in notary offices
sections.body: The advancement of electronic platforms, service centers, and digital systems requires notary offices to be prepared to operate in increasingly technological environments. Key technological needs include: high-performance corporate servers, structured and secure networks, backup and data recovery environments, digital security solutions, and professional IT infrastructure management.

sections.heading: Technology as an ally of notarial and registry activity
sections.body: The digitization of notary services should be seen not only as a regulatory obligation but as an opportunity to improve management and increase operational efficiency. With proper use of technology, it is possible to reduce operational risks, increase team productivity, improve document security, ensure greater service availability, and strengthen the reliability of the office.

sections.heading: The importance of specialized IT support for notary offices
sections.body: IT infrastructure management requires specialized technical knowledge. Many notary offices choose to work with companies specialized in information technology to ensure their systems are always updated, protected, and functioning correctly. This support includes activities such as server monitoring, network management, digital security implementation, preventive maintenance, and specialized technical support.

sections.heading: WMTi Information Technology: IT infrastructure for notary offices
sections.body: WMTi Information Technology has over 15 years of experience in technological infrastructure solutions for companies and institutions that depend on secure and reliable environments. Services offered include: corporate server deployment, enterprise network infrastructure, network security with firewall, Microsoft 365 solutions, and monitoring and specialized technical support.

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.body: CNJ Provision 213 reinforces the importance of technological modernization in notary offices. To meet regulatory requirements and ensure service continuity, notary offices need to invest in secure, stable, and professionally managed IT infrastructure. Technology has become an essential element to ensure efficiency, legal certainty, and quality in public service.

faq.q: What is CNJ Provision 213?
faq.a: It is a provision from the National Council of Justice that establishes guidelines for technological modernization, system integration, and information security for notary offices in Brazil.

faq.q: What are the main technological requirements for notary offices?
faq.a: Reliable servers, stable corporate networks, backup systems, firewall, access control, and constant IT environment monitoring.

faq.q: Does WMTi serve notary offices?
faq.a: Yes. WMTi has over 15 years of experience in IT infrastructure and serves notary offices with server, network, security, and specialized technical support solutions.

faq.q: How to ensure technological compliance for my notary office?
faq.a: By working with a specialized company that evaluates your current infrastructure, identifies improvement points, and implements solutions appropriate to regulatory requirements.

provimento-213-cnj-desafios-tecnologia-c.cta: Does your notary office need secure and reliable IT infrastructure? WMTi specializes in technology solutions for notary offices. Request a free assessment.

ransomware-em-hospitais-como-proteger.title: Ransomware in hospitals: how to protect your healthcare institution
ransomware-em-hospitais-como-proteger.excerpt: Hospitals are the primary target of ransomware attacks in Brazil. Learn how to protect electronic health records and ensure continuity of care.
ransomware-em-hospitais-como-proteger.metaTitle: Ransomware in Hospitals: How to Protect Your Institution | WMTi
ransomware-em-hospitais-como-proteger.metaDescription: Hospitals are the primary target of ransomware attacks. Learn how to protect medical records, medical systems, and ensure continuity of care.

sections.body: Hospitals and healthcare institutions have become the preferred target of digital criminals. According to security reports, the healthcare sector experienced a 74% increase in ransomware attacks in the last two years. The reason is simple: hospitals cannot stop, and therefore are more likely to pay ransoms to recover their data.

sections.heading: Why hospitals are priority targets
sections.body: Hospitals operate 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. An interruption in systems can mean delays in surgeries, loss of access to electronic health records, and inability to issue medical reports. Criminals know that the pressure for continuity of care makes hospitals more vulnerable to extortion. Additionally, medical data is worth up to 10 times more than financial data on the black market.

sections.heading: How ransomware enters the hospital network
sections.body: The most common attack vectors are phishing emails targeting employees, exploitation of vulnerabilities in outdated systems, and poorly configured remote access. Once inside the network, the malware spreads laterally, encrypting servers, workstations, and even backup systems connected to the network.

sections.heading: Essential protection measures
sections.body: Protection against ransomware requires a layered approach: pfSense firewall with IDS/IPS to block malicious traffic, network segmentation to isolate medical equipment, automated offline backup with periodic verification, constant updating of operating systems and applications, and team training to identify phishing attempts.

sections.heading: The role of backup in recovery
sections.body: A well-structured backup is the last line of defense against ransomware. The 3-2-1 strategy (3 copies, 2 different media, 1 offsite) ensures that even if ransomware compromises the local backup, there is a secure copy outside the network. WMTi implements automated backup with integrity verification and periodic restoration tests.

sections.heading: Incident response plan
sections.body: Every hospital needs a documented ransomware incident response plan. The plan should include immediate isolation of infected machines, communication with IT team and management, scope assessment of the attack, restoration from verified backup, and forensic analysis to prevent recurrence.

faq.q: Should hospitals pay the ransomware ransom?
faq.a: Security experts and authorities recommend not paying. Payment funds crime and does not guarantee data recovery. The best protection is having up-to-date and tested backup.

faq.q: How long does it take to recover a hospital from a ransomware attack?
faq.a: With adequate backup, recovery can take 24 to 72 hours. Without backup, the process can take weeks or result in permanent data loss.

faq.q: Which hospital systems are most targeted?
faq.a: Electronic health records (EHR), PACS (medical images), hospital management systems (ERP), and file servers are the main targets.

ransomware-em-hospitais-como-proteger.cta: Is your healthcare institution protected against ransomware? WMTi performs security diagnostics for hospitals and clinics, identifying vulnerabilities and implementing layered protections.

vazamento-dados-clinicas-medicas-lgpd.title: Data breaches in medical clinics: risks and how to prevent them
vazamento-dados-clinicas-medicas-lgpd.excerpt: Medical clinics handle sensitive patient data. Understand the risks of data leaks and how LGPD requires adequate protection.
vazamento-dados-clinicas-medicas-lgpd.metaTitle: Data Breaches in Medical Clinics: Risks and LGPD | WMTi
vazamento-dados-clinicas-medicas-lgpd.metaDescription: Medical clinics handle sensitive patient data. Understand the risks of data leaks and how to comply with LGPD.

sections.body: Medical clinics store extremely sensitive information: diagnoses, test results, treatment histories, and personal patient data. The General Data Protection Law (LGPD) classifies health data as sensitive, requiring enhanced protection measures.

sections.heading: Sensitive medical data under LGPD
sections.body: LGPD defines health data as sensitive personal data (Art. 5, II). This means clinics need a specific legal basis for processing this data, technical and administrative security measures, and may face fines of up to 2% of revenue in case of a breach.

sections.heading: Main causes of data leaks in clinics
sections.body: The most common leaks occur due to weak or shared passwords among employees, lack of encryption on workstations and servers, improper disposal of equipment containing data, uncontrolled access to electronic health records, and absence of firewall and network segmentation.

sections.heading: Consequences of a medical data breach
sections.body: Besides ANPD fines, a breach can result in damage to the clinic reputation, lawsuits from affected patients, loss of insurance contracts and partnerships, and direct impact on patient trust.

sections.heading: How to protect patient data
sections.body: Adequate protection involves role-based access control (RBAC), data encryption in transit and at rest, pfSense firewall with audit logs, encrypted backup with external storage, strong password policy with multi-factor authentication, and regular staff training.

faq.q: Do small clinics also need to comply with LGPD?
faq.a: Yes. LGPD applies to any organization that processes personal data, regardless of size. Small clinics need the same protection measures.

faq.q: What is the fine for medical data breaches?
faq.a: ANPD can apply fines of up to 2% of the company revenue, limited to R$50 million per violation, plus sanctions such as data blocking.

faq.q: How to prove LGPD compliance?
faq.a: Through documentation of security policies, data processing records, impact reports (RIPD), and evidence of implemented technical measures.

vazamento-dados-clinicas-medicas-lgpd.cta: Need to protect patient data in your clinic? WMTi implements information security infrastructure in compliance with LGPD.

backup-para-cartorios-estrategias-segura.title: Backup for notary offices: secure strategies to protect digital archives
backup-para-cartorios-estrategias-segura.excerpt: Notary offices need reliable backup to protect notarial acts and records. Discover the best strategies.
backup-para-cartorios-estrategias-segura.metaTitle: Backup for Notary Offices: Secure Strategies for Digital Archives | WMTi
backup-para-cartorios-estrategias-segura.metaDescription: Notary offices need reliable backup to protect notarial acts and records. Discover the best strategies.

sections.body: Notary offices are responsible for the custody and preservation of documents with public faith. The digitization of archives and the use of electronic systems have made backup a critical necessity, especially considering the requirements of CNJ Provision 74.

sections.heading: Legal backup requirements for notary offices
sections.body: CNJ Provision 74 requires notary offices to maintain backup copies of digital archives, stored in a location different from the office. CNJ Provision 213 reinforces these requirements, establishing minimum information security standards for notary offices.

sections.heading: Risks of not having adequate backup
sections.body: Data loss in a notary office can mean destruction of notarial acts without possibility of recovery, service interruption to the population, personal liability of the notary or registrar, and administrative sanctions from the court of justice.

sections.heading: 3-2-1 backup strategy for notary offices
sections.body: WMTi recommends the 3-2-1 strategy adapted for notary offices: 3 copies of data (original + 2 backups), 2 different media (RAID server + external storage), 1 offsite copy (encrypted cloud backup or external vault). All backups are automated, encrypted, and verified daily.

sections.heading: Local backup with Dell PowerEdge server
sections.body: The Dell PowerEdge server with RAID provides the first layer of protection, with disk redundancy that protects against hardware failure. WMTi configures automatic snapshots that allow restoring data from any point in time.

sections.heading: Periodic restoration tests
sections.body: A backup that has never been tested is not reliable. WMTi performs periodic restoration tests to ensure data can be effectively recovered when needed. Test reports are documented for audit purposes.

faq.q: How often should a notary office backup be performed?
faq.a: Incremental backup should be daily, with weekly full backup. For high-volume notary offices, backup can be done every hour.

faq.q: Is cloud backup safe for notary offices?
faq.a: Yes, as long as it uses AES-256 encryption, TLS transmission, and certified datacenter storage. WMTi configures cloud backup with all these protection layers.

faq.q: How to ensure backup is working?
faq.a: Through automated monitoring with failure alerts and periodic restoration tests. WMTi monitors client backups and notifies immediately in case of failure.

backup-para-cartorios-estrategias-segura.cta: Does your notary office need a reliable backup strategy? WMTi implements automated backup solutions with encryption and restoration tests.

firewall-pfsense-para-empresas-protecao-.title: pfSense firewall for businesses: complete protection against intrusions
firewall-pfsense-para-empresas-protecao-.excerpt: pfSense is an enterprise-grade open-source firewall solution that protects your network against external and internal threats.
firewall-pfsense-para-empresas-protecao-.metaTitle: pfSense Firewall for Businesses: Complete Protection | WMTi
firewall-pfsense-para-empresas-protecao-.metaDescription: pfSense is an enterprise firewall solution that protects your network against intrusions, ransomware, and unauthorized access.

sections.body: The firewall is the first line of defense for the corporate network. pfSense is an enterprise-grade open-source solution that offers advanced security features without the prohibitive costs of proprietary firewalls like Cisco ASA or Fortinet.

sections.heading: What is pfSense
sections.body: pfSense is a FreeBSD-based firewall operating system used by companies of all sizes worldwide. It offers stateful firewall, VPN, IDS/IPS, proxy, load balancing, and much more, with an intuitive web interface for management.

sections.heading: pfSense security features
sections.body: Key features include: deep packet inspection (DPI), intrusion detection and prevention system (IDS/IPS) with Snort or Suricata, country-based traffic blocking (GeoIP), web content filtering, site-to-site and client-to-site VPN, and complete audit logs.

sections.heading: pfSense vs proprietary firewalls
sections.body: While proprietary firewalls charge annual licenses of R$5,000 to R$50,000, pfSense is free and offers equivalent functionality. WMTi deploys pfSense on dedicated appliances with ongoing support and monitoring.

sections.heading: Corporate use cases
sections.body: pfSense is ideal for protecting hospital networks (medical equipment segmentation), notary offices (Provision 213 compliance), law firms (confidential data protection), and businesses in general (access control and VPN for remote work).

faq.q: Is pfSense reliable for business use?
faq.a: Yes. pfSense is used by thousands of companies worldwide, including government and military organizations. Its FreeBSD base is recognized for stability and security.

faq.q: Do I need dedicated hardware for pfSense?
faq.a: Yes, we recommend dedicated appliances to ensure performance and reliability. WMTi provides and configures appropriate hardware for your network size.

faq.q: Does pfSense replace antivirus?
faq.a: No. pfSense protects the network perimeter, while antivirus protects endpoints. Both are necessary for effective layered security.

firewall-pfsense-para-empresas-protecao-.cta: Protect your network with pfSense configured by specialists. WMTi deploys, monitors, and maintains your corporate firewall.

ataques-ciberneticos-escritorios-advocac.title: Cyber attacks on law firms: how to protect yourself
ataques-ciberneticos-escritorios-advocac.excerpt: Law firms are frequent targets of hackers due to handling confidential information. See how to shield your operations.
ataques-ciberneticos-escritorios-advocac.metaTitle: Cyber Attacks on Law Firms | WMTi
ataques-ciberneticos-escritorios-advocac.metaDescription: Law firms are frequent targets of hackers. Learn how to protect confidential data and ensure digital security.

sections.body: Law firms are attractive targets for digital criminals because they handle highly confidential information: contracts, court cases, client financial data, and confidential legal strategies. A successful attack can compromise the firm reputation and professional activity.

sections.heading: Why law firms are vulnerable
sections.body: Most law firms invest little in IT security. Outdated computers, weak passwords, no firewall, and irregular backup are common problems. Additionally, lawyers frequently access files from public Wi-Fi networks without VPN.

sections.heading: Most common attack types
sections.body: The most frequent attacks against legal offices include: targeted phishing (spear phishing) with fake court emails, ransomware that encrypts cases and documents, interception of communications on unsecured networks, and social engineering to gain system access.

sections.heading: Impact of an attack on a law firm
sections.body: The consequences can be devastating: loss of essential cases and documents, breach of professional confidentiality, liability for client damages, OAB and ANPD fines, and irreparable loss of professional credibility.

sections.heading: Recommended protection measures
sections.body: WMTi recommends: pfSense firewall with VPN for secure remote access, encrypted automated backup, Microsoft 365 with advanced email protection, document access control by confidentiality level, disk encryption on notebooks and desktops, and team training to recognize threats.

faq.q: Do small firms also suffer cyber attacks?
faq.a: Yes. Small firms are even easier targets because they generally have fewer protections implemented. The size of the firm does not reduce the value of the data it holds.

faq.q: How to protect confidential legal documents?
faq.a: With data encryption, permission-based access control, encrypted backup, VPN for remote access, and firewall to protect the network perimeter.

faq.q: Are lawyers required to protect client data under LGPD?
faq.a: Yes. Lawyers are controllers of their clients personal data and need to implement technical and administrative protection measures as required by LGPD.

ataques-ciberneticos-escritorios-advocac.cta: Protect your law firm against cyber attacks. WMTi implements secure infrastructure with firewall, VPN, and backup for legal offices.

servidores-dell-poweredge-seguranca-dado.title: Dell PowerEdge servers: data security for businesses
servidores-dell-poweredge-seguranca-dado.excerpt: Discover how Dell PowerEdge servers offer native security features like TPM, Secure Boot, and iDRAC to protect your data.
servidores-dell-poweredge-seguranca-dado.metaTitle: Dell PowerEdge Servers: Data Security for Businesses | WMTi
servidores-dell-poweredge-seguranca-dado.metaDescription: Discover how Dell PowerEdge servers offer native security features to protect corporate data.

sections.body: Dell PowerEdge servers are designed with security as a priority. From firmware to hardware, each layer includes protection features that ensure the integrity of corporate data against physical and digital threats.

sections.heading: Hardware-level security
sections.body: Dell PowerEdge servers include TPM 2.0 (Trusted Platform Module) for hardware encryption, Secure Boot that prevents execution of unauthorized firmware, chassis with intrusion detection, and Silicon Root of Trust that verifies BIOS integrity at every startup.

sections.heading: iDRAC: secure remote management
sections.body: iDRAC (Integrated Dell Remote Access Controller) allows secure remote server management, including console access even with the operating system frozen, automated firmware updates, temperature, ventilation, and power monitoring, and automatic hardware failure alerts.

sections.heading: RAID for data protection
sections.body: Dell PERC RAID controllers protect against disk failure with mirroring (RAID 1) or distributed parity (RAID 5/6). In case of a disk failure, the server continues operating normally while the disk is hot-swapped.

sections.heading: Dell PowerEdge for different segments
sections.body: WMTi recommends the appropriate model for each scenario: PowerEdge T350 for small businesses and offices, PowerEdge R750xs for hospitals and clinics with virtualization, PowerEdge R650 for notary offices with high data volume, and PowerEdge T550 for companies that need a robust tower server.

faq.q: What is the lifespan of a Dell PowerEdge server?
faq.a: With proper maintenance, a Dell PowerEdge server can operate for 7 to 10 years. Dell offers up to 5 years of warranty with ProSupport.

faq.q: Do I need a climate-controlled room for the server?

lgpd-para-clinicas-e-hospitais-guia-prat.title: LGPD for clinics and hospitals: practical compliance guide
lgpd-para-clinicas-e-hospitais-guia-prat.excerpt: Clinics and hospitals must adapt their IT infrastructure to comply with LGPD. See the step-by-step guide to ensure compliance.
lgpd-para-clinicas-e-hospitais-guia-prat.metaTitle: LGPD for Clinics and Hospitals: Practical Guide | WMTi
lgpd-para-clinicas-e-hospitais-guia-prat.metaDescription: Practical compliance guide with LGPD for clinics and hospitals. Learn how to adapt your IT infrastructure.

sections.body: LGPD imposes specific obligations on healthcare institutions that process sensitive personal data. Clinics and hospitals need to adapt not only administrative processes but especially their IT infrastructure to ensure technical protection of patient data.

sections.heading: Sensitive data in healthcare
sections.body: LGPD classifies as sensitive all health-related data: medical records, exam results, prescriptions, diagnoses, treatment information, and hospitalization history. Processing this data requires specific consent or qualified legal justification.

sections.heading: LGPD technical requirements for IT
sections.body: From an infrastructure standpoint, LGPD requires: data encryption at rest and in transit, granular access control to systems, audit logs for personal data access, backup with protection against accidental or intentional loss, and ability to fulfill data subject requests (access, correction, deletion).

sections.heading: Compliant IT infrastructure
sections.body: WMTi implements IT infrastructure that meets LGPD requirements: Dell PowerEdge servers with native encryption, pfSense firewall with audit logs, Active Directory with role-based access control, automated encrypted backup, and 24/7 monitoring with security alerts.

sections.heading: Data Protection Impact Report (RIPD)
sections.body: Clinics and hospitals must prepare a RIPD for high-risk processing activities. Adequate IT infrastructure provides the technical evidence needed for this report, demonstrating the security measures implemented.

faq.q: Can clinics be fined under LGPD?
faq.a: Yes. ANPD can apply fines of up to 2% of revenue, limited to R$50 million per violation, plus sanctions such as data blocking and public disclosure of the infraction.

faq.q: How to adapt electronic health records to LGPD?
faq.a: The electronic health record system must have profile-based access control, audit logs, encryption, backup, and ability to fulfill patient requests about their data.

faq.q: Does WMTi help with LGPD compliance?
faq.a: Yes. WMTi implements the technical layer of data protection: servers, firewall, backup, encryption, and access control as required by LGPD.

lgpd-para-clinicas-e-hospitais-guia-prat.cta: Need to adapt your clinic IT to LGPD? WMTi implements secure infrastructure in compliance with data protection legislation.

como-ransomware-ataca-cartorios.title: How ransomware attacks notary offices and what to do for protection
como-ransomware-ataca-cartorios.excerpt: Notary offices are among the most vulnerable targets for ransomware. Understand the attack vector and protection measures.
como-ransomware-ataca-cartorios.metaTitle: How Ransomware Attacks Notary Offices and How to Protect | WMTi
como-ransomware-ataca-cartorios.metaDescription: Notary offices are vulnerable targets for ransomware. Understand the attack vector and essential protection measures.

sections.body: Notary offices hold invaluable digital archives: deeds, certificates, powers of attorney, and notarial acts. Losing this data to a ransomware attack can have irreversible legal consequences and compromise the public faith of the acts.

sections.heading: Notary offices in the crosshairs of cybercrime
sections.body: Notary offices are attractive targets for three reasons: they possess high-value data, they generally invest little in IT security, and they cannot stop operating. These characteristics make notary offices prone to paying ransoms to recover encrypted data.

sections.heading: Most common attack vectors
sections.body: Attacks on notary offices usually start with phishing emails with malicious attachments disguised as summons or official letters, exploitation of RDP ports exposed to the internet, contaminated USB drives brought by employees, and vulnerabilities in outdated software.

sections.heading: Multi-layered protection for notary offices
sections.body: WMTi implements layered protection: pfSense firewall with IDS/IPS and external RDP blocking, managed antivirus on all workstations, network segmentation separating critical systems, offline backup with media rotation, and team training to identify threats.

sections.heading: Compliance with Provision 213
sections.body: CNJ Provision 213 requires notary offices to implement information security measures. WMTi solutions fully meet these requirements, including firewall, backup, access control, and monitoring.

faq.q: What to do if the notary office is attacked by ransomware?
faq.a: Immediately disconnect equipment from the network, do not pay the ransom, contact the IT team, and restore data from offline backup.

faq.q: Does cloud backup protect against ransomware?
faq.a: Partially. Backup must be isolated from the main network so ransomware cannot reach it. Cloud backup with versioning is an additional protection layer.

faq.q: Does Provision 213 require a firewall?
faq.a: Yes. Provision 213 requires perimeter protection measures, including a configured and monitored firewall.

como-ransomware-ataca-cartorios.cta: Protect your notary office against ransomware. WMTi implements layered protection in compliance with CNJ Provision 213.

falhas-infraestrutura-ti-hospitais.title: IT infrastructure failures in hospitals: risks and solutions
falhas-infraestrutura-ti-hospitais.excerpt: A server failure in a hospital can compromise patient care. Learn about risks and how to implement high availability.
falhas-infraestrutura-ti-hospitais.metaTitle: IT Infrastructure Failures in Hospitals: Risks and Solutions | WMTi
falhas-infraestrutura-ti-hospitais.metaDescription: Server failures in hospitals compromise patient care. Learn about risks and how to implement high availability.

sections.body: In a hospital, IT infrastructure is not just operational support — it is a critical element for patient safety. When a server fails, the consequences go beyond business interruption: they can directly affect patient care and treatment.

sections.heading: Critical hospital systems
sections.body: Systems that cannot go down include: Electronic Health Record (EHR), PACS (medical image storage and distribution), Hospital Management System (ERP), pharmacy system with prescription control, and laboratory system with exam results.

sections.heading: Impact of IT failures on hospital care
sections.body: When a server goes down in a hospital: doctors lose access to patient history, surgeries may be postponed, prescriptions cannot be verified electronically, imaging exams become unavailable, and patient admission and discharge stop.

sections.heading: Causes of failures in hospital infrastructure
sections.body: The most common causes are: outdated servers without RAID redundancy, overloaded network without proper segmentation, lack of UPS (uninterruptible power supply), absent or insufficient backup, and lack of preventive maintenance.

sections.heading: High availability solutions
sections.body: WMTi implements high-availability infrastructure for hospitals: Dell PowerEdge servers with RAID and redundant power supplies, network with structured cabling and redundancy, pfSense firewall with automatic failover, automated backup with verified restoration, and 24/7 monitoring with prioritized alerts.

faq.q: How to prevent server failure in a hospital?
faq.a: With servers with RAID redundancy, preventive maintenance, 24/7 monitoring, and tested backup. WMTi implements all these layers.

faq.q: What to do when the hospital server goes down?
faq.a: Activate the incident response plan, communicate with clinical teams, initiate restoration from backup, and investigate the root cause.

faq.q: Does the hospital need a secondary server?
faq.a: For critical environments, yes. Virtualization with automatic failover ensures that a secondary server takes over immediately in case of primary failure.

falhas-infraestrutura-ti-hospitais.cta: Ensure IT availability in your hospital. WMTi implements high-availability infrastructure with monitoring and verified backup.

backup-automatizado-clinicas-medicas.title: Automated backup for medical clinics: protect exams and medical records
backup-automatizado-clinicas-medicas.excerpt: Medical images and electronic health records need automated and tested backup. Learn how to implement it in your clinic.
backup-automatizado-clinicas-medicas.metaTitle: Automated Backup for Medical Clinics: Protect Exams and Records | WMTi
backup-automatizado-clinicas-medicas.metaDescription: Medical images and electronic records need automated and tested backup. Learn how to implement it in your clinic.

sections.body: Medical clinics generate critical data daily: clinical images, medical reports, exam results, and patient records. The loss of this data can have legal, financial, and reputational consequences for the clinic.

sections.heading: Types of data that need backup in a clinic
sections.body: Critical data includes: DICOM images (X-rays, ultrasounds, CT scans), electronic health records, clinical reports and prescriptions, financial and billing data, and employee and payroll records.

sections.heading: Risks of not having automated backup
sections.body: Without automated backup: human error can leave the clinic without a recent copy, ransomware can encrypt all data including manual backup, equipment failure can destroy irreplaceable data, and the clinic may violate LGPD for not protecting patient data.

sections.heading: Automated backup implementation
sections.body: WMTi configures automated backup for clinics: daily incremental backup with minimal impact on performance, weekly full backup for complete restoration, encrypted backup on external storage (onsite), encrypted cloud backup (offsite), and automated monitoring with failure alerts.

sections.heading: Backup for DICOM images
sections.body: Medical images in DICOM format require special attention: large files that demand adequate storage, long retention (minimum 20 years per CFM), need for integrity verification, and access compatibility with PACS systems.

faq.q: How much storage is needed for clinic backup?
faq.a: It depends on exam volume. Clinics with X-ray and CT generate more data. WMTi sizes storage according to each clinic profile.

faq.q: Can backup slow down the clinic systems?
faq.a: No, if properly configured. Incremental backup during off-peak hours has minimal impact on performance.

faq.q: How long should patient records be kept?
faq.a: CFM establishes that medical records must be kept for at least 20 years. Backup must consider this long-term retention.

backup-automatizado-clinicas-medicas.cta: Protect your institution electronic health records. WMTi implements secure infrastructure with access control and encrypted backup.

segmentacao-rede-hospitalar-seguranca.title: Hospital network segmentation: why it is essential for security
segmentacao-rede-hospitalar-seguranca.excerpt: Unsegmented hospital networks expose medical equipment and patient data. Understand how segmentation protects your institution.
segmentacao-rede-hospitalar-seguranca.metaTitle: Hospital Network Segmentation: Essential for Security | WMTi
segmentacao-rede-hospitalar-seguranca.metaDescription: Unsegmented hospital networks expose medical equipment and patient data. Learn how segmentation protects your institution.

sections.body: Hospital networks are complex environments with thousands of connected devices: computers, medical equipment, IP cameras, access control, and IoT devices. Without proper segmentation, a security breach in one device can compromise the entire network.

sections.heading: Why hospital networks need segmentation
sections.body: In an unsegmented network: a compromised computer can access medical equipment, ransomware can spread to all connected devices, medical IoT devices are exposed to external attacks, and there is no traffic isolation between different departments.

sections.heading: Hospital VLAN architecture
sections.body: WMTi implements VLAN architecture for hospitals: administrative VLAN (computers and printers), medical VLAN (medical systems and records), IoMT VLAN (connected medical equipment), guest VLAN (visitors and patients), and management VLAN (servers and network equipment).

sections.heading: Firewall between segments
sections.body: pfSense controls traffic between VLANs: only necessary communication is allowed between segments, all inter-VLAN traffic is logged, IDS/IPS rules detect anomalies, and automatic alerts for suspicious traffic.

sections.heading: Benefits of segmentation
sections.body: A segmented network provides: ransomware containment (does not spread to the entire network), protection of medical equipment against external attacks, regulatory compliance (LGPD, ANVISA), simplified traffic monitoring, and better network performance.

faq.q: Is network segmentation mandatory for hospitals?
faq.a: There is no specific law requiring it, but information security best practices and LGPD compliance requirements make segmentation practically essential for hospitals.

faq.q: Does segmentation affect network speed?
faq.a: No. When properly implemented, segmentation can actually improve performance by reducing broadcast traffic on each segment.

faq.q: Can segmentation be done on an existing network?
faq.a: Yes. WMTi plans and executes segmentation in existing networks without interrupting hospital operations.

segmentacao-rede-hospitalar-seguranca.cta: Protect your hospital network with professional segmentation. WMTi implements VLAN architecture with pfSense firewall.

phishing-em-escritorios-advocacia-como-e.title: Phishing in law firms: how to avoid email scams
phishing-em-escritorios-advocacia-como-e.excerpt: Phishing attacks are the gateway for intrusions in legal offices. Learn to identify and block these threats.
phishing-em-escritorios-advocacia-como-e.metaTitle: Phishing in Law Firms: How to Avoid Scams | WMTi
phishing-em-escritorios-advocacia-como-e.metaDescription: Phishing attacks are the gateway for intrusions in legal offices. Learn to identify and block these threats.

sections.body: Phishing is the most common cyber attack against law firms. Criminals send emails that appear to come from courts, bar associations, or clients, inducing lawyers and staff to click malicious links or open contaminated attachments.

sections.heading: How phishing targets law firms
sections.body: Attacks targeting law firms are sophisticated: emails mimicking court notifications with case numbers, fake bar association messages requesting credential updates, emails from supposed clients with contaminated document attachments, and fake invoices from actual suppliers.

sections.heading: Consequences of a successful phishing attack
sections.body: When a law firm employee falls for phishing: credentials can be stolen and used to access case systems, ransomware can be installed encrypting all firm files, confidential client data can be exfiltrated, and the firm may be held liable for data breaches under LGPD.

sections.heading: Technical protection measures
sections.body: WMTi implements technical protections against phishing: Microsoft 365 with Advanced Threat Protection, pfSense firewall with malicious URL filtering, DNS filtering to block known phishing domains, email authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and centralized antivirus with automatic updates.

sections.heading: Team training
sections.body: Technology alone is not enough. WMTi recommends: periodic training to identify phishing emails, phishing simulations to test team awareness, clear reporting procedures for suspicious emails, and security culture reinforced by firm management.

faq.q: How to identify a phishing email?
faq.a: Check the sender (often a similar domain), urgency in the message, suspicious links (hover to see the real URL), and unexpected attachments.

faq.q: Does antivirus protect against phishing?
faq.a: Partially. Antivirus can block known malware, but advanced phishing attacks may evade detection. Layered protection is necessary.

faq.q: What to do if I clicked a phishing link?
faq.a: Change your passwords immediately, notify the IT team, scan the computer for malware, and monitor for suspicious activity in your accounts.

phishing-em-escritorios-advocacia-como-e.cta: Protect your law firm against phishing. WMTi implements email security with Microsoft 365 and pfSense firewall.

redundancia-internet-clinicas-hospitais.title: Internet redundancy in clinics and hospitals: why it is mandatory
redundancia-internet-clinicas-hospitais.excerpt: An internet outage in a hospital can paralyze critical systems. See how to implement redundancy with automatic failover.
redundancia-internet-clinicas-hospitais.metaTitle: Internet Redundancy in Clinics and Hospitals: Mandatory | WMTi
redundancia-internet-clinicas-hospitais.metaDescription: An internet outage in a hospital can paralyze critical systems. See how to implement redundancy with automatic failover.

sections.body: Modern clinics and hospitals depend on internet for virtually every operation: electronic health record access, telemedicine, integration with insurance companies, cloud systems, and digital communications. A single internet link represents a critical point of failure.

sections.heading: What depends on internet in a hospital
sections.body: Critical systems that depend on connectivity: cloud electronic health records, telemedicine platforms, integration with insurance companies and SUS, cloud-based laboratory systems, pharmaceutical supply systems, and inter-unit communication (VoIP, Teams).

sections.heading: Risks of internet outage
sections.body: When the internet goes down in a hospital: cloud systems become inaccessible, telemedicine consultations are interrupted, insurance authorizations cannot be processed, electronic prescriptions cannot be sent, and patient check-in and discharge are delayed.

sections.heading: Redundancy implementation with pfSense
sections.body: WMTi configures internet redundancy with pfSense: two or more internet links from different providers, automatic failover (less than 30 seconds), load balancing during normal operation, automatic alerts in case of link failure, and monitoring of link quality (latency, packet loss).

sections.heading: Choosing providers
sections.body: For effective redundancy: use providers with different technologies (fiber + radio, or fiber + satellite), with independent physical paths, different entry points in the building, and contracts with guaranteed SLA.

faq.q: How much does internet redundancy cost?
faq.a: The cost of a second link varies, but is a fraction of the loss caused by an outage. WMTi helps choose the best cost-benefit option.

faq.q: Does the switchover cause any disruption?
faq.a: With pfSense configured by WMTi, failover occurs in less than 30 seconds. Most users do not even notice the switch.

faq.q: Can I use 4G/5G as backup?
