ransomware-wannacry-licoes-para-empresas.title: WannaCry: lessons from the largest ransomware attack for Brazilian companies
ransomware-wannacry-licoes-para-empresas.excerpt: WannaCry affected hospitals and businesses worldwide. Learn the lessons from this attack and how to prevent it from happening to your company.
ransomware-wannacry-licoes-para-empresas.metaTitle: WannaCry: Lessons from the Largest Ransomware Attack | WMTi
ransomware-wannacry-licoes-para-empresas.metaDescription: WannaCry affected hospitals and businesses worldwide. Learn the lessons and how to prevent it.

sections.body: In May 2017, WannaCry ransomware infected over 200,000 computers in 150 countries in just a few hours. Hospitals, factories, telecommunications companies, and governments were paralyzed. The attack exploited a known Windows vulnerability that had a patch available for two months.

sections.heading: How WannaCry worked
sections.body: WannaCry exploited the EternalBlue vulnerability in Windows SMB protocol. It spread automatically across the network without requiring user action. Each infected computer scanned the network for other vulnerable machines, creating an exponential cascade of infections.

sections.heading: Impact on hospitals
sections.body: The UK National Health Service (NHS) was one of the hardest hit: 80 healthcare organizations affected, surgeries cancelled, ambulances redirected, and patients without access to test results. The estimated cost exceeded £92 million.

sections.heading: Lessons for Brazilian companies
sections.body: WannaCry exposed critical failures: systems without security updates are easy targets, internal network segmentation could have limited the spread, offline backup would have allowed recovery without paying ransom, and firewalls with IDS/IPS could have detected the spread.

sections.heading: How to protect against the next WannaCry
sections.body: WMTi implements the lessons from WannaCry: automated updates on all workstations and servers, pfSense firewall with IDS/IPS to detect exploits, network segmentation to contain lateral spread, offline backup immune to network attacks, and 24/7 monitoring to detect anomalous behavior.

faq.q: Can WannaCry still infect computers?
faq.a: Yes. There are still computers with unpatched Windows connected to the internet. WannaCry variants continue circulating and exploiting the same vulnerability.

faq.q: Do updated systems still need a firewall?
faq.a: Yes. Updates fix known vulnerabilities, but new ones are discovered daily. The firewall is an additional protection layer.

faq.q: Did WannaCry affect companies in Brazil?
faq.a: Yes. Brazilian companies in the healthcare, telecommunications, and industrial sectors were affected, including hospitals that had to revert to paper processes.

ransomware-wannacry-licoes-para-empresas.cta: Protect your company against the next cyber attack. WMTi implements the lessons from WannaCry with layered security.

vpn-segura-para-escritorios-advocacia.title: Secure VPN for law firms: protected remote access
vpn-segura-para-escritorios-advocacia.excerpt: Lawyers need to access cases and documents remotely with security. See how to implement corporate VPN with pfSense.
vpn-segura-para-escritorios-advocacia.metaTitle: Secure VPN for Law Firms: Protected Remote Access | WMTi
vpn-segura-para-escritorios-advocacia.metaDescription: Lawyers need secure remote access to cases and documents. See how to implement corporate VPN with pfSense.

sections.body: Remote work became a reality for law firms. Lawyers need to access case systems, documents, and databases from home, courts, and during travel. Without a VPN, this access occurs over unprotected connections, exposing confidential data.

sections.heading: Why lawyers need VPN
sections.body: Lawyers routinely access confidential data from external locations: court hearings where they need to check processes, client meetings requiring document access, home office work, and travel between court branches. Without VPN, this data travels unencrypted over public networks.

sections.heading: Risks of access without VPN
sections.body: Without VPN: data can be intercepted on public Wi-Fi networks, credentials can be captured by man-in-the-middle attacks, confidential documents can be exposed, and the firm may violate LGPD for not protecting data in transit.

sections.heading: VPN implementation with pfSense
sections.body: WMTi configures corporate VPN with pfSense: OpenVPN or IPsec with military-grade encryption, individual certificates for each user, two-factor authentication (2FA), access log for compliance, and automatic connection on firm-managed devices.

sections.heading: Security best practices for remote access
sections.body: Beyond VPN, WMTi recommends: disk encryption on notebooks, screen lock policy after inactivity, prohibition of saving firm files on personal devices, security training for remote access, and 24/7 monitoring of VPN connections.

faq.q: Does VPN slow down the connection?
faq.a: Minimally. With pfSense and adequate hardware, the speed reduction is imperceptible for normal use like document access and email.

faq.q: Can I use VPN on my cell phone?
faq.a: Yes. WMTi configures VPN for iOS and Android, allowing secure access from mobile devices.

faq.q: Is VPN mandatory for LGPD compliance?
faq.a: LGPD requires technical measures to protect personal data. VPN is one of the recommended measures for protecting data in transit.

vpn-segura-para-escritorios-advocacia.cta: Implement secure VPN for your law firm. WMTi configures VPN with pfSense and advanced protection for remote access.

como-proteger-prontuario-eletronico.title: How to protect electronic patient records against attacks
como-proteger-prontuario-eletronico.excerpt: Electronic health records contain sensitive data protected by LGPD. Learn how to secure access and ensure data integrity.
como-proteger-prontuario-eletronico.metaTitle: How to Protect Electronic Patient Records | WMTi
como-proteger-prontuario-eletronico.metaDescription: Electronic health records contain sensitive LGPD-protected data. Learn how to secure access and ensure data integrity.

sections.body: The electronic health record (EHR) is the most critical and sensitive system in any healthcare institution. It contains complete patient histories: diagnoses, treatments, exams, prescriptions, and personal information. Protecting this data is both a legal obligation and an ethical responsibility.

sections.heading: Why EHR is a prime target
sections.body: Medical data has high value on the black market: complete health records are worth up to 10x more than credit card data, they can be used for identity fraud and insurance scams, and once leaked, they cannot be changed (unlike a password).

sections.heading: Most common vulnerabilities
sections.body: The most frequent EHR vulnerabilities include: shared login among multiple professionals, absence of access logs, EHR accessible from any computer without restrictions, unencrypted local database, and backup without encryption or access control.

sections.heading: Multi-layered protection
sections.body: WMTi implements comprehensive EHR protection: Active Directory with individual login per professional, role-based access control (doctor, nurse, receptionist), complete audit logs (who accessed what and when), database encryption at rest and in transit, and pfSense firewall with network segmentation.

sections.heading: Backup and disaster recovery for EHR
sections.body: The EHR needs special backup: automated daily incremental backup, weekly full backup with integrity verification, encrypted offsite copy, periodic restoration tests, and minimum 20-year retention as required by CFM.

faq.q: Can EHR be accessed remotely?
faq.a: Yes, but only through VPN with encryption and two-factor authentication. WMTi configures secure remote access for physicians.

faq.q: What to do if patient data is leaked?
faq.a: Notify the ANPD and affected patients, investigate the root cause, implement corrective measures, and document the entire process.

faq.q: How long should electronic records be kept?
faq.a: CFM establishes that medical records must be kept for at least 20 years. Backup must consider this long-term retention.

como-proteger-prontuario-eletronico.cta: Protect your institution electronic health records. WMTi implements secure infrastructure with access control and encrypted backup.

servidor-dedicado-vs-nuvem-para-empresas.title: Dedicated server vs cloud: which is better for your business?
servidor-dedicado-vs-nuvem-para-empresas.excerpt: Find out when to choose a local Dell PowerEdge server or the cloud, considering security, performance, and costs.
servidor-dedicado-vs-nuvem-para-empresas.metaTitle: Dedicated Server vs Cloud: Which Is Better? | WMTi
servidor-dedicado-vs-nuvem-para-empresas.metaDescription: Find out when to choose a local server or cloud, considering security, performance, and costs.

sections.body: The choice between a dedicated local server and cloud infrastructure is one of the most important IT decisions for any company. Each option has advantages and disadvantages that must be evaluated according to the business profile and needs.

sections.heading: Advantages of a dedicated local server
sections.body: A local server offers total control over hardware and data, predictable performance without internet dependency, fixed cost without consumption surprises, and facilitated compliance with regulations requiring data on national territory. It is ideal for companies with systems that require low latency and high performance.

sections.heading: Advantages of cloud infrastructure
sections.body: The cloud offers on-demand scalability, native geographic redundancy, no hardware investment, and access from anywhere. It is ideal for companies with remote teams that need flexibility.

sections.heading: Hybrid model: the best of both worlds
sections.body: For many companies, the best option is the hybrid model: local Dell PowerEdge server for critical systems (ERP, database), Microsoft 365 in the cloud for email and collaboration, and cloud backup as a second protection layer. WMTi designs custom hybrid architectures.

sections.heading: Total cost of ownership analysis (TCO)
sections.body: A local server has high acquisition cost and predictable maintenance. The cloud has monthly costs that can grow with usage. Over a 5-year horizon, the local server is generally more economical for constant workloads, while the cloud is more advantageous for variable loads.

faq.q: Is a local server more secure than the cloud?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Security depends on configuration and maintenance. A poorly configured local server is more vulnerable than a well-managed cloud, and vice versa.

faq.q: Can I migrate from local server to cloud later?
faq.a: Yes. Migration can be done gradually. WMTi plans migrations without downtime, maintaining normal operations during the process.

faq.q: What option does WMTi recommend?
faq.a: It depends on the company profile. WMTi performs a technical and financial assessment to recommend the best architecture for each case.

servidor-dedicado-vs-nuvem-para-empresas.cta: Need to decide between local server and cloud? WMTi performs technical and financial analysis to recommend the best solution.

politica-seguranca-informacao-empresas.title: Information security policy: how to create one for your company
politica-seguranca-informacao-empresas.excerpt: An information security policy is the first step to protecting corporate data. See how to create yours.
politica-seguranca-informacao-empresas.metaTitle: Information Security Policy for Businesses | WMTi
politica-seguranca-informacao-empresas.metaDescription: An information security policy is the first step to protecting corporate data. See how to create yours.

sections.body: An information security policy (ISP) is the document that defines rules, procedures, and responsibilities for protecting company data and systems. Without an ISP, each employee acts according to their own criteria, creating security gaps.

sections.heading: Why your company needs an ISP
sections.body: An ISP establishes behavioral standards for all employees, defines responsibilities in case of incidents, meets LGPD and sector regulation requirements, reduces risks of data leaks and attacks, and facilitates decision-making in crisis situations.

sections.heading: What the ISP should contain
sections.body: An effective ISP covers: password policy (complexity, periodic changes, sharing prohibition), access policy (who accesses what, principle of least privilege), backup policy (frequency, testing, storage), device usage policy (BYOD, mobile devices), incident response procedures, and information classification (public, internal, confidential, restricted).

sections.heading: Technical implementation of the ISP
sections.body: The ISP needs technical support to work: Active Directory with GPOs to enforce password policies, pfSense firewall to enforce internet access policies, automated backup per defined policy, managed antivirus on all workstations, and compliance monitoring.

sections.heading: Training and awareness
sections.body: The ISP only works if employees know and understand it. WMTi recommends initial training for all staff, annual refresher courses, phishing simulations to test awareness, and clear communication of consequences for non-compliance.

faq.q: Do small companies need an ISP?
faq.a: Yes. Companies of any size handle data that needs protection. A simple and objective ISP is better than none.

faq.q: Who should prepare the ISP?
faq.a: The ISP should be prepared by the IT team with management support. WMTi assists in defining the technical layer of the ISP.

faq.q: Does the ISP need to be updated?
faq.a: Yes. The ISP should be reviewed annually or whenever there are significant changes in the infrastructure or company processes.

politica-seguranca-informacao-empresas.cta: Need to implement a security policy with technical support? WMTi configures the infrastructure that sustains your ISP.

backup-3-2-1-estrategia-para-empresas.title: 3-2-1 backup strategy: the gold standard for data protection
backup-3-2-1-estrategia-para-empresas.excerpt: The 3-2-1 backup rule ensures your data survives any disaster. Learn how to apply it in your business.
backup-3-2-1-estrategia-para-empresas.metaDescription: The 3-2-1 rule ensures your data survives any disaster. Learn how to apply it in your business.

sections.body: The 3-2-1 backup strategy is considered the gold standard for data protection by security experts worldwide. Simple to understand and highly effective, this rule ensures your data survives virtually any type of disaster.

sections.heading: What is the 3-2-1 rule
sections.body: The rule is simple: 3 copies of data (original + 2 backups), 2 different media (e.g., RAID server + external storage), 1 offsite copy (outside the physical location of the company). This combination protects against hardware failure, human error, ransomware attacks, and physical disasters.

sections.heading: The first copy: protected original data
sections.body: Original data should be on a server with RAID for disk failure protection. WMTi uses Dell PowerEdge servers with RAID 1, 5, or 6 depending on the required protection level.

sections.heading: The second copy: local backup
sections.body: Local backup on separate storage allows quick restoration. It should be automated with integrity verification, and ideally on equipment physically separated from the main server.

sections.heading: The third copy: offsite backup
sections.body: Offsite backup protects against fire, theft, or flooding at the location. It can be encrypted cloud or external vault. WMTi implements automated offsite backup with AES-256 encryption.

sections.heading: Evolution: the 3-2-1-1-0 rule
sections.body: The modern version of the rule adds: 1 offline copy (disconnected from the network, immune to ransomware) and 0 restoration errors (verified periodic tests). WMTi implements this advanced version for clients who need maximum protection.

faq.q: Is the 3-2-1 rule sufficient against ransomware?
faq.a: The basic rule may not be, as ransomware can reach network-connected backups. The 3-2-1-1-0 version includes an offline copy that is immune to ransomware.

faq.q: How much does it cost to implement 3-2-1 backup?
faq.a: The cost varies according to data volume. WMTi sizes the solution according to the company needs and budget.

faq.q: How often should backup be tested?
faq.a: Restoration tests should be done at least monthly. WMTi automates and documents these tests.

backup-3-2-1-estrategia-para-empresas.cta: Implement 3-2-1 backup in your company. WMTi configures automated backup with verification and restoration tests.

monitoramento-rede-prevencao-ataques.title: Network monitoring: how to prevent attacks before they happen
monitoramento-rede-prevencao-ataques.excerpt: Proactive monitoring identifies threats and anomalies before they cause damage. Learn about essential tools and practices.
monitoramento-rede-prevencao-ataques.metaDescription: Proactive monitoring identifies threats before they cause damage. Learn about essential practices.

sections.body: Proactive network monitoring is the ability to identify and resolve problems before they impact business operations. Instead of reacting to failures, the IT team anticipates problems and acts preventively.

sections.heading: Reactive vs proactive monitoring
sections.body: In the reactive model, the IT team only acts when something stops working. In the proactive model, automatic alerts identify anomalies before failure: disks with defective sectors before complete failure, abnormal bandwidth consumption indicating a possible attack, SSL certificates about to expire, and services with increasing memory consumption.

sections.heading: What to monitor on the corporate network
sections.body: Critical monitoring items include: server health (CPU, RAM, disk, temperature), backup status (success/failure), internet link availability, firewall logs (intrusion attempts), printer and network equipment status, and license and certificate validity.

sections.heading: Monitoring tools
sections.body: WMTi uses professional tools like Zabbix and Grafana for real-time visual monitoring, email and SMS alerts, historical performance reports, and personalized dashboards per client.

sections.heading: Incident response
sections.body: When monitoring identifies a problem, the response process includes: automatic alert to the technical team, severity classification, remote corrective action when possible, escalation to on-site service if necessary, and documentation of the incident and solution.

faq.q: Does monitoring work 24 hours?
faq.a: Yes. The monitoring system operates 24/7, with automatic alerts to the on-call team for critical incidents outside business hours.

faq.q: Do I need special software for monitoring?
faq.a: Yes. WMTi installs and configures monitoring agents on client equipment at no additional licensing cost.

faq.q: How much does the monitoring service cost?
faq.a: Monitoring is generally included in WMTi IT support contracts. Contact us for a personalized quote.

monitoramento-rede-prevencao-ataques.cta: Monitor your network 24/7 with WMTi. Identify problems before they affect your operations.

lgpd-para-cartorios-adequacao-necessaria.title: LGPD for notary offices: necessary compliance for data protection
lgpd-para-cartorios-adequacao-necessaria.excerpt: Notary offices handle sensitive personal data and need to comply with LGPD. See what changes in the IT infrastructure.
lgpd-para-cartorios-adequacao-necessaria.metaTitle: LGPD for Notary Offices: Required Compliance | WMTi
lgpd-para-cartorios-adequacao-necessaria.metaDescription: Notary offices handle sensitive personal data and need to comply with LGPD. See what changes in IT.

sections.body: Notary offices process thousands of citizens personal and sensitive data daily: ID numbers, birth, marriage, and death certificates, property deeds, and powers of attorney. LGPD and CNJ provisions require rigorous technical protection of this data.

sections.heading: Personal data in notary offices
sections.body: Notary offices are large-scale personal data controllers. They process identification data (name, ID), biometric data (fingerprints), property data (deeds, contracts), and civil status data (certificates). LGPD requires these data be protected with technical measures proportional to the risk.

sections.heading: Provision 213 and LGPD: convergence
sections.body: CNJ Provision 213 converged information security requirements for notary offices with LGPD principles, requiring: information security policy, system access control, encrypted backup, firewall and antivirus, and processing activity records.

sections.heading: Technical measures for compliance
sections.body: WMTi implements the technical compliance layer: Dell PowerEdge server with encryption and RAID, pfSense firewall with audit logs, Active Directory with profile-based access control, automated encrypted backup (3-2-1), and 24/7 security monitoring.

sections.heading: Risks of non-compliance
sections.body: Notary offices that do not comply with LGPD face: ANPD fines up to R$50 million, court of justice sanctions, lawsuits from data subjects, severe reputational damage, and possible loss of delegation.

faq.q: Do notary offices need a DPO?
faq.a: CNJ recommends that notary offices designate a data protection officer (DPO), which can be the principal or a designated professional.

faq.q: Does Provision 213 replace LGPD?
faq.a: No. Provision 213 complements LGPD with specific requirements for notary offices. Both must be complied with.

faq.q: What is the compliance deadline?
faq.a: LGPD is already in force and sanctions can be applied. Provision 213 also has specific deadlines that should be verified with the local court of justice.

lgpd-para-cartorios-adequacao-necessaria.cta: Comply your notary office IT with LGPD and Provision 213. WMTi implements all necessary infrastructure.

ataques-ddos-como-proteger-empresa.title: DDoS attacks: how to protect your company against downtime
ataques-ddos-como-proteger-empresa.excerpt: DDoS attacks can take down your company services for hours. Learn how a properly configured firewall can block these threats.
ataques-ddos-como-proteger-empresa.metaTitle: DDoS Attacks: How to Protect Your Company | WMTi
ataques-ddos-como-proteger-empresa.metaDescription: DDoS attacks can take down your company services. Learn how a configured firewall blocks these threats.

sections.body: Distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks overwhelm company infrastructure with malicious traffic, making services unavailable to customers and employees. Companies that depend on internet to operate are especially vulnerable.

sections.heading: How a DDoS attack works
sections.body: In a DDoS attack, thousands of infected computers (botnet) send simultaneous requests to the target. The traffic volume is so large that the server or internet link cannot process it, causing unavailability.

sections.heading: Business impact
sections.body: A DDoS attack can cause: unavailability of websites and web systems, email and online service interruption, loss of sales and productivity, damage to company reputation, and costs with emergency mitigation.

sections.heading: Protection with pfSense
sections.body: pfSense offers features to mitigate DDoS attacks: connection rate limiting per IP, country-based traffic blocking (GeoIP), firewall rules to filter malformed packets, syncookies for SYN flood protection, and integration with IP reputation lists.

sections.heading: Complementary strategies
sections.body: For more robust protection, WMTi recommends: CDN with DDoS protection for public websites, internet link redundancy, traffic monitoring with anomaly alerts, and DDoS incident response plan.

faq.q: Do small companies suffer DDoS attacks?
faq.a: Yes. DDoS attacks are increasingly cheap to execute and can target companies of any size, including as a form of extortion.

faq.q: Can pfSense block all DDoS attacks?
faq.a: pfSense mitigates most attacks. For very large volumetric attacks, additional protection at the ISP layer may be necessary.

faq.q: How to know if I am under a DDoS attack?
faq.a: Signs include extreme internet slowness, service unavailability without apparent reason, and high bandwidth consumption on the firewall.

ataques-ddos-como-proteger-empresa.cta: Protect your company against DDoS attacks. WMTi configures pfSense firewall with denial of service protection.

ransomware-como-servico-ameaca-crescente.title: Ransomware as a Service (RaaS): the growing threat to businesses
ransomware-como-servico-ameaca-crescente.excerpt: The RaaS model allows criminals without technical knowledge to launch ransomware attacks. Understand this threat and how to defend against it.
ransomware-como-servico-ameaca-crescente.metaTitle: Ransomware as a Service (RaaS): Growing Threat | WMTi
ransomware-como-servico-ameaca-crescente.metaDescription: The RaaS model enables ransomware attacks by non-technical criminals. Understand and defend yourself.

sections.body: Ransomware as a Service (RaaS) has democratized cybercrime. Just as legitimate companies offer Software as a Service (SaaS), criminal groups now sell ready-to-use ransomware kits with control panels, technical support, and profit sharing.

sections.heading: How the RaaS model works
sections.body: RaaS developers create the ransomware and make it available on the dark web. Affiliates (attackers) pay a subscription or share a percentage of ransoms collected. The model includes: ready-to-use ransomware with a control panel, instructions for distributing the malware, technical support for affiliates, and automatic cryptocurrency payment processing.

sections.heading: Why RaaS is more dangerous
sections.body: Before RaaS, ransomware attacks required advanced technical knowledge. Now anyone with basic computer skills can launch an attack. This has multiplied the number of attackers and victims exponentially.

sections.heading: Most active RaaS groups
sections.body: Groups like LockBit, BlackCat (ALPHV), and Cl0p have been responsible for major attacks globally. They target companies of all sizes, including hospitals, law firms, and notary offices.

sections.heading: How to protect against RaaS
sections.body: Protection against RaaS follows the same principles as any ransomware: pfSense firewall with IDS/IPS, network segmentation, 3-2-1-1-0 backup with offline copy, email security with advanced phishing protection, employee training, and 24/7 monitoring.

faq.q: How much does a RaaS kit cost?
faq.a: On the dark web, kits range from $40 to thousands of dollars, or operate on a profit-sharing model (typically 20-30% for the developer).

faq.q: Can law enforcement shut down RaaS groups?
faq.a: International operations have disrupted some groups, but new ones emerge quickly. Technical protection remains the best defense.

faq.q: Are Brazilian companies being targeted by RaaS?
faq.a: Yes. Brazil is among the most attacked countries in Latin America, with companies in healthcare, legal, and financial sectors being priority targets.

ransomware-como-servico-ameaca-crescente.cta: Protect your company against Ransomware as a Service. WMTi implements layered security to block these threats.

recuperacao-desastres-ti-plano-pratico.title: IT disaster recovery: how to create a practical plan
recuperacao-desastres-ti-plano-pratico.excerpt: A disaster recovery plan ensures your company is back up and running quickly after incidents. See how to create yours.
recuperacao-desastres-ti-plano-pratico.metaTitle: IT Disaster Recovery: Practical Plan | WMTi
recuperacao-desastres-ti-plano-pratico.metaDescription: A disaster recovery plan ensures your company is back quickly after incidents. See how to create yours.

sections.body: A disaster recovery plan (DRP) is a documented set of procedures that enable a company to resume IT operations after a critical incident. Without a DRP, a server failure, ransomware attack, or natural disaster can paralyze the company for days or weeks.

sections.heading: What constitutes an IT disaster
sections.body: IT disasters include: ransomware or malware attacks, complete server failure, fire or flooding in the server room, theft of IT equipment, prolonged power outages, and accidental or malicious data corruption.

sections.heading: Essential DRP elements
sections.body: An effective DRP must define: recovery time objective (RTO) — maximum acceptable downtime, recovery point objective (RPO) — maximum acceptable data loss, team roles and responsibilities, step-by-step recovery procedures, and internal and external communication plan.

sections.heading: DRP technical infrastructure
sections.body: WMTi implements the technical infrastructure to support the DRP: verified automated backup (3-2-1-1-0), server virtualization with snapshots, infrastructure documentation updated, redundant internet with automatic failover, and tested restoration environment.

sections.heading: Testing and updating the DRP
sections.body: A DRP that has never been tested is unreliable. WMTi recommends: semi-annual restoration simulations, annual review of the plan, update after any significant infrastructure change, and documentation of all tests and results.

faq.q: Do all companies need a DRP?
faq.a: Any company that depends on IT to operate needs a DRP. The plan complexity should be proportional to the business size and criticality.

faq.q: What is the difference between DRP and backup?
faq.a: Backup is one component of the DRP. The plan also includes procedures, responsibilities, communications, and recovery objectives.

faq.q: How long does it take to create a DRP?
faq.a: An initial DRP can be created in 2 to 4 weeks. WMTi assists in creating and testing the plan.

recuperacao-desastres-ti-plano-pratico.cta: Create a disaster recovery plan for your company. WMTi implements the infrastructure and procedures to ensure quick recovery.

seguranca-email-corporativo-ameacas-comu.title: Corporate email security: common threats and how to fight them
seguranca-email-corporativo-ameacas-comu.excerpt: Corporate email is the attack vector most exploited by hackers. Learn about the most common threats and how to protect your company.
seguranca-email-corporativo-ameacas-comu.metaTitle: Corporate Email Security: Common Threats | WMTi
seguranca-email-corporativo-ameacas-comu.metaDescription: Corporate email is the most exploited attack vector. Learn about common threats and how to protect your company.

sections.body: Corporate email remains the primary entry point for cyber attacks. Over 90% of successful attacks begin with a phishing email. Protecting corporate email is not optional — it is a fundamental security necessity.

sections.heading: Most common email threats
sections.body: Main threats include: phishing (fake messages to steal credentials), spear phishing (personalized attacks targeting specific individuals), business email compromise (BEC) impersonating executives, malware attachments disguised as invoices or documents, and malicious links to credential-harvesting sites.

sections.heading: Specific risks for accounting firms
sections.body: Accounting firms are particularly vulnerable: they receive files from multiple clients, handle financial data and tax information, have predictable deadlines (tax submissions) that attackers exploit, and a successful attack can compromise data from all clients.

sections.heading: Protection with Microsoft 365
sections.body: Microsoft 365 offers advanced email protection: Exchange Online Protection (EOP) for spam and malware filtering, Advanced Threat Protection (ATP) for zero-day threat analysis, Safe Links that scan URLs in real time, Safe Attachments that detonate files in sandbox, and Data Loss Prevention (DLP) to prevent leaks.

sections.heading: Complementary measures
sections.body: Beyond Microsoft 365, WMTi implements: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC for email authentication, pfSense firewall with DNS-level malicious URL blocking, periodic phishing training and simulations, and email access policies (2FA, managed devices only).

faq.q: Is free email (Gmail, Outlook.com) secure for businesses?
faq.a: No. Free email does not offer the security and management features needed for the corporate environment. Microsoft 365 Business provides advanced protection.

faq.q: What is multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
faq.a: MFA requires a second verification factor beyond the password (code by SMS, app, or key). It blocks 99.9% of automated attacks.

faq.q: How to train employees against phishing?
faq.a: With periodic training, real-case examples, phishing simulations, and a clear reporting culture for suspicious emails.

seguranca-email-corporativo-ameacas-comu.cta: Protect your company email with Microsoft 365 and pfSense. WMTi implements advanced security for corporate communications.

virtualizacao-servidores-seguranca-perfo.title: Server virtualization: more security and performance for your company
virtualizacao-servidores-seguranca-perfo.excerpt: Virtualization allows running multiple servers on a single hardware, with isolation and snapshots for quick recovery.
virtualizacao-servidores-seguranca-perfo.metaTitle: Server Virtualization: Security and Performance | WMTi
virtualizacao-servidores-seguranca-perfo.metaDescription: Virtualization allows running multiple servers on a single hardware, with isolation and snapshots for quick recovery.

sections.body: Server virtualization allows running multiple virtual servers (VMs) on a single physical hardware. Each VM operates independently, with its own operating system and resources, providing isolation, flexibility, and efficiency.

sections.heading: Benefits of virtualization
sections.body: Virtualization offers: better utilization of physical hardware resources, isolation between services (ERP, email, files), instant snapshots for quick recovery, facilitated migration between physical servers, and reduction in hardware quantity and energy consumption.

sections.heading: Virtualization and security
sections.body: Virtualization enhances security: isolation between VMs prevents one compromised service from affecting others, snapshots allow quick rollback after incidents, it is possible to create isolated test environments, and network segmentation between VMs adds an extra protection layer.

sections.heading: Recommended platforms
sections.body: WMTi works with leading virtualization platforms: Proxmox VE (open-source, enterprise-grade), VMware vSphere (market leader), and Microsoft Hyper-V (integrated with Windows Server). The choice depends on the client profile, budget, and requirements.

sections.heading: Virtualization on Dell PowerEdge
sections.body: Dell PowerEdge servers are ideal for virtualization: powerful multi-core processors, high-capacity RAM (up to 2TB in some models), RAID storage for VM data protection, iDRAC for remote management, and compatibility with all major hypervisors.

faq.q: Is virtualization secure?
faq.a: Yes. Virtualization adds security layers through isolation between VMs and quick recovery capabilities through snapshots.

faq.q: Can I virtualize my current server?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. WMTi evaluates the current server and plans the migration to a virtual environment without downtime.

faq.q: Does virtualization affect performance?
faq.a: With proper hardware sizing, the impact is minimal. Modern hypervisors are highly optimized and can even improve performance through better resource management.

virtualizacao-servidores-seguranca-perfo.cta: Virtualize your company servers. WMTi designs, implements, and manages virtual environments on Dell PowerEdge.

ciberseguranca-para-pequenas-empresas.title: Cybersecurity for small businesses: essential protection guide
ciberseguranca-para-pequenas-empresas.excerpt: Small businesses are easy targets because they invest little in security. See essential measures that fit your budget.
ciberseguranca-para-pequenas-empresas.metaTitle: Cybersecurity for Small Businesses: Essential Guide | WMTi
ciberseguranca-para-pequenas-empresas.metaDescription: Small businesses are easy targets for investing little in security. See essential measures that fit your budget.

sections.body: Small businesses are increasingly becoming targets of cyber attacks. The misconception that hackers only target large corporations puts thousands of small businesses at risk every day. In reality, 43% of cyber attacks target small businesses, and 60% of those attacked close within six months.

sections.heading: Why small businesses are targets
sections.body: Small businesses are attractive targets because: they invest little in security, creating easy entry points, they often lack dedicated IT staff, employees receive no cybersecurity training, backup is often non-existent or untested, and they can serve as a gateway to attack larger partner companies.

sections.heading: Essential measures for small budgets
sections.body: Effective cybersecurity does not require large investments. Essential measures include: pfSense firewall (free software with WMTi configuration and support), strong password policy with multi-factor authentication, automated and tested backup (3-2-1 rule), operating system and application updates, and basic security training for all employees.

sections.heading: Quick wins in security
sections.body: Measures that can be implemented immediately: enable MFA on all accounts (email, banking, systems), remove admin access from employee computers, configure automatic updates on all machines, implement the 3-2-1 backup rule, and install and configure pfSense firewall.

sections.heading: When to seek professional help
sections.body: Some situations require professional IT support: setting up and monitoring the firewall, implementing network segmentation, configuring automated enterprise backup, evaluating and hardening existing infrastructure, and incident response and forensic investigation.

faq.q: How much does cybersecurity cost for a small business?
faq.a: Essential measures like pfSense (free), MFA (free), and automated backup can be implemented with minimal investment. WMTi offers affordable plans for small businesses.

faq.q: Can I handle security internally?
faq.a: Basic measures yes, but firewall configuration, monitoring, and incident response require specialized knowledge. Outsourcing this service is more cost-effective for small businesses.

faq.q: What is the most common attack on small businesses?
faq.a: Phishing (fraudulent emails) is the most common attack, followed by ransomware. Both can be mitigated with training and technical protections.

ciberseguranca-para-pequenas-empresas.cta: Protect your small business against cyber attacks. WMTi offers affordable security solutions sized for your business.

auditoria-seguranca-ti-por-que-fazer.title: IT security audit: why your company needs to do it
auditoria-seguranca-ti-por-que-fazer.excerpt: A security audit identifies vulnerabilities before they are exploited. Understand the process and benefits.
auditoria-seguranca-ti-por-que-fazer.metaTitle: IT Security Audit: Why Your Company Needs It | WMTi
auditoria-seguranca-ti-por-que-fazer.metaDescription: A security audit identifies vulnerabilities before they are exploited. Understand the process and benefits.

sections.body: An IT security audit is a systematic evaluation of the company technological infrastructure to identify vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and compliance gaps. It is the most effective way to understand the real state of your company security.

sections.heading: What the audit evaluates
sections.body: A comprehensive audit covers: firewall configuration and effectiveness, network segmentation and access controls, backup status and restoration tests, operating system and application updates, password policies and access management, compliance with LGPD and sector regulations, and physical security of IT equipment.

sections.heading: Benefits of a security audit
sections.body: An audit provides: clear view of existing vulnerabilities, prioritized action plan for corrections, baseline for measuring security improvements, compliance evidence for audits and certifications, and risk reduction through proactive identification.

sections.heading: What happens after the audit
sections.body: WMTi delivers a detailed report with: identified vulnerabilities classified by severity, specific remediation recommendations, estimated cost and effort for each correction, implementation timeline, and comparison with industry best practices.

sections.heading: Audit frequency
sections.body: WMTi recommends: annual comprehensive audit, quarterly vulnerability assessments, post-incident review audits, and audits before major infrastructure changes.

faq.q: How long does a security audit take?
faq.a: A comprehensive audit typically takes 1 to 2 weeks, depending on the infrastructure size and complexity.

faq.q: Will the audit interrupt company operations?
faq.a: No. The audit is conducted with minimal impact on daily operations. Most evaluations are non-intrusive.

faq.q: Is the audit mandatory?
faq.a: It is not legally mandatory for most companies, but LGPD requires periodic evaluation of security measures, making the audit practically essential.

auditoria-seguranca-ti-por-que-fazer.cta: Discover the real state of your company IT security. WMTi performs comprehensive audits with detailed reports and action plans.

criptografia-dados-empresariais-guia.title: Enterprise data encryption: practical implementation guide
criptografia-dados-empresariais-guia.excerpt: Encryption protects data in transit and at rest from unauthorized access. See how to implement it in your infrastructure.
criptografia-dados-empresariais-guia.metaTitle: Enterprise Data Encryption: Implementation Guide | WMTi
criptografia-dados-empresariais-guia.metaDescription: Encryption protects data in transit and at rest from unauthorized access. See how to implement it.

sections.body: Data encryption is one of the most effective measures for protecting corporate information. It transforms readable data into an unintelligible code that can only be decoded with the correct key. Even if data is stolen, it remains inaccessible without the decryption key.

sections.heading: Types of encryption for businesses
sections.body: Companies need encryption at multiple levels: encryption at rest (data stored on servers, disks, and backups), encryption in transit (data traveling over the network and internet), full disk encryption (protection in case of theft or loss of equipment), and database encryption (specific protection for sensitive records).

sections.heading: Encryption at rest
sections.body: Data stored on servers, NAS, and backup must be encrypted. Dell PowerEdge servers offer Self-Encrypting Drives (SED) with automatic hardware encryption. For backup, WMTi implements AES-256 encryption before sending data to external storage or cloud.

sections.heading: Encryption in transit
sections.body: Data traveling over the network must be protected: VPN with IPsec or OpenVPN encryption for remote access, TLS/SSL for web connections and email, HTTPS for all internal and external web applications, and WPA3 for corporate wireless networks.

sections.heading: Implementing encryption in your company
sections.body: WMTi implements encryption at all levels: BitLocker or LUKS for disk encryption on workstations, Dell SED for server disk encryption, pfSense VPN with military-grade encryption, TLS certificates for internal and external services, and encrypted backup with controlled key management.

faq.q: Does encryption affect performance?
faq.a: With modern hardware, the performance impact is minimal. Dell PowerEdge servers with SED drives perform encryption at the hardware level without CPU overhead.

faq.q: Is encryption mandatory under LGPD?
faq.a: LGPD requires appropriate technical measures to protect personal data. Encryption is one of the most recommended and effective measures.

faq.q: What happens if I lose the encryption key?
faq.a: Without the key, data is permanently inaccessible. WMTi implements key management procedures with secure backup of encryption keys.

criptografia-dados-empresariais-guia.cta: Implement data encryption in your company. WMTi configures encryption at rest, in transit, and backup with professional management.

equipamentos-medicos-conectados-riscos-s.title: Connected medical equipment: security risks in hospitals
equipamentos-medicos-conectados-riscos-s.excerpt: Medical IoT equipment expands the attack surface in hospitals. Know the risks and how to segment the network for protection.
equipamentos-medicos-conectados-riscos-s.metaTitle: Connected Medical Equipment: Security Risks in Hospitals | WMTi
equipamentos-medicos-conectados-riscos-s.metaDescription: Medical IoT equipment expands the attack surface in hospitals. Know the risks and how to segment the network.

sections.body: The Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has brought extraordinary advances to healthcare, but also created new security risks. Patient monitors, infusion pumps, MRI machines, and CT scanners connected to the network significantly expand the attack surface in hospitals.

sections.heading: The IoMT landscape in hospitals
sections.body: A modern hospital may have hundreds of network-connected medical devices: ICU multiparameter monitors, smart infusion pumps, imaging devices (digital X-ray, CT scanner, MRI), mechanical ventilators with telemetry, and digital electrocardiographs.

sections.heading: IoMT security risks
sections.body: Connected medical devices present specific risks: many run outdated operating systems without patches, firmware is rarely updated by the manufacturer, default credentials are frequently not changed, communication protocols may not be encrypted, and the attack surface grows with each connected device.

sections.heading: Consequences of an attack on medical equipment
sections.body: An attack on connected medical equipment can: interrupt patient monitoring in ICU, alter dosages on infusion pumps, make imaging devices unavailable, serve as an entry point for ransomware on the network, and compromise patient exam data.

sections.heading: Protection: segmentation is key
sections.body: The most important measure is network segmentation: medical equipment should be on an isolated VLAN, pfSense firewall controlling traffic between segments, only necessary communication allowed between VLANs, anomalous traffic monitoring on the IoMT network, and updated inventory of all connected devices.

faq.q: Can medical equipment be hacked?
faq.a: Yes. Security researchers have already demonstrated vulnerabilities in infusion pumps, pacemakers, and imaging devices. Network segmentation is essential to mitigate this risk.

faq.q: Is the equipment manufacturer responsible for security?
faq.a: Partially. The manufacturer is responsible for firmware patches, but the security of the network where the equipment operates is the hospital responsibility.

faq.q: How to monitor IoMT equipment?
faq.a: With network monitoring tools that identify devices, monitor traffic, and alert about anomalous behavior.

equipamentos-medicos-conectados-riscos-s.cta: Protect your hospital medical equipment. WMTi implements network segmentation and monitoring for IoMT.

guia-completo-infraestrutura-ti-empresas.title: Complete guide to IT infrastructure for businesses
guia-completo-infraestrutura-ti-empresas.excerpt: Understand the main elements of an efficient IT infrastructure and how companies can improve their technological environments.
guia-completo-infraestrutura-ti-empresas.metaTitle: Complete Guide to IT Infrastructure for Businesses | WMTi
guia-completo-infraestrutura-ti-empresas.metaDescription: Understand the main elements of an efficient IT infrastructure and how companies can improve their technological environments.

sections.body: IT infrastructure is the technological foundation that supports a modern company operations. Computers, servers, corporate networks, systems, and cloud solutions work together to ensure internal processes function efficiently and securely.

sections.heading: Common problems in companies without adequate infrastructure
sections.body: Companies that do not have a well-structured technological infrastructure frequently face problems such as system slowness, network failures, data loss, and growth difficulties.

sections.heading: Main components of business IT infrastructure
sections.body: Corporate servers responsible for storing data and running systems. Corporate networks that connect computers and devices within the company. Digital security to protect data and systems against attacks. Backup systems to prevent information loss. Infrastructure monitoring to identify problems before they cause disruptions.

sections.heading: Benefits of investing in IT infrastructure
sections.body: Investing in IT infrastructure enables greater productivity, security, and operational stability. WMTi Information Technology has over 15 years of experience helping companies implement reliable technological environments.

faq.q: What is IT infrastructure?

firewall-empresarial-sua-empresa-precisa.title: Business firewall: does your company really need one?
firewall-empresarial-sua-empresa-precisa.excerpt: Understand how firewalls protect corporate networks against attacks and digital intrusions.
firewall-empresarial-sua-empresa-precisa.metaTitle: Business Firewall: Does Your Company Really Need One? | WMTi
firewall-empresarial-sua-empresa-precisa.metaDescription: Understand how firewalls protect corporate networks against attacks and digital intrusions.

sections.body: With the increase in digital threats, protecting the corporate network has become a priority for companies of all sizes. A business firewall functions as a security barrier between the company internal network and the internet.

sections.heading: How a business firewall works
sections.body: It analyzes data traffic and blocks suspicious or unauthorized access. Without a corporate firewall, companies can be vulnerable to various types of attacks.

sections.heading: Threats blocked by the firewall
sections.body: Network intrusions. Data theft. Ransomware attacks. Unauthorized system access.

sections.heading: Advanced features
sections.body: Modern firewalls also allow internet access control, traffic monitoring, and protection against malicious software. Implementing an adequate firewall significantly increases IT infrastructure security.

faq.q: What is a business firewall?
faq.a: It is a security solution that monitors and controls data traffic between the internal network and the internet.

faq.q: Does my company need a firewall?
faq.a: Yes. Any company connected to the internet is exposed to threats that a firewall can block.

faq.q: Which firewall does WMTi recommend?
faq.a: pfSense is one of the most reliable and widely used solutions in corporate environments.

firewall-empresarial-sua-empresa-precisa.cta: Protect your company network with a professional firewall. Talk to WMTi.

servidor-caiu-empresa-o-que-fazer.title: Server crashed at your company: what to do
servidor-caiu-empresa-o-que-fazer.excerpt: See the most common causes of failures in business servers and how to avoid this problem.
servidor-caiu-empresa-o-que-fazer.metaTitle: Server Crashed at Your Company: What to Do | WMTi
servidor-caiu-empresa-o-que-fazer.metaDescription: See the most common causes of failures in business servers and how to avoid this problem.

sections.body: When a corporate server stops working, the entire company operation can be affected. Systems become unavailable, files may be inaccessible, and employees cannot work normally.

sections.heading: Common signs of server failure
sections.body: Systems that do not load. Extreme network slowness. Errors when accessing files. Computers that cannot connect to the server.

sections.heading: Most common causes
sections.body: Hardware problems. Processing overload. Corporate network failures. Cyber attacks.

sections.heading: How to prevent failures
sections.body: Companies can reduce this risk with server monitoring, preventive maintenance, and adequate infrastructure.

faq.q: What to do when the company server goes down?
faq.a: Check the hardware, verify the network, and contact specialized technical support immediately.

faq.q: How to prevent server downtime?
faq.a: With proactive monitoring, preventive maintenance, and quality infrastructure.

servidor-caiu-empresa-o-que-fazer.cta: Is your server having problems? WMTi can help. Request technical support.
